title: The Secret Society That Controlled the World's Electricity
description:
In the winter of 1924, far from the public eye, a group of powerful industrialists quietly gathered in a smoke-filled room in Geneva, Switzerland. Representing the world's leading lighting conglomerates, these men were not there to celebrate innovation.
This is the dark business history behind The Phoebus Cartel, planned obsolescence, and lightbulb conspiracies.

title: How One Family Fooled the World into Buying Worthless Rocks
description:
The diamond engagement ring was not an ancient tradition. It was engineered by De Beers and an ad agency that turned a common stone into a test of love, status, and masculinity.
This is the dark business history of the De Beers diamond cartel, artificial scarcity, and the marketing campaign that convinced millions to buy the illusion.

title: The Poisoned Formula: The Corporate Giant That Stole Africa's Markets

In the 1970s, Nestle sold baby formula as modern progress in some of the poorest markets on Earth. Free samples, white coats, and aggressive marketing helped turn infant feeding into a corporate dependency trap.

title: The Secret Society That Controlled the World's Electricity
description:
In 1924, the world’s biggest lightbulb companies formed a cartel with one quiet goal: make bulbs burn out faster. The Phoebus Cartel turned planned failure into a business model, and its shadow still reaches into the products we buy today.
This is the dark business history behind planned obsolescence, the 1,000-hour rule, and the secret agreement that made better engineering bad for business.

title: The Toxic Empire: The Family That Hooked America on Pain
description:
They were the royalty of medical philanthropy, their family name carved into the marble walls of the world's most prestigious museums. From the Metropolitan Museum of Art to the Louvre in Paris, the Sackler family name was synonymous with culture, wealth, and high society.
This is the dark business history behind The Sackler family, Purdue Pharma, and the OxyContin epidemic.

title: The Silent Killer: How a Chemical Giant Poisoned a Town
description:
Take a look at the frying pan in your kitchen. Sleek, non-stick, and completely harmless. Or so you have been told.
This is the dark business history behind DuPont, Teflon, and the C8 water contamination scandal.

title: The Company That Patented Nature: How a Corporate Giant Controlled the World's Seeds
description:
It is a quiet, dusty sunset on a family farm in America's heartland. The farmer is inside having dinner, completely unaware of the dark shadow walking along the edge of his soybean fields.
This is the dark business history behind Monsanto's patenting of genetically modified crops and their aggressive enforcement against independent family farmers.

title: The Golden Scam: The Man Who Sold a Fake Gold Mine
description:
Deep within the steaming, impenetrable jungles of East Kalimantan, Borneo, a small Canadian exploration company claimed to have made a discovery that would rewrite the history of wealth. In 1993, Bre-X Minerals announced they had stumbled upon the largest gold deposit ever found in human history—a volcanic structure containing over two hundred million ounces of pure gold.
This is the dark business history behind The Bre-X mining scandal in the Indonesian jungle.

title: The Secret Monopoly That Controls Everything You Wear
description:
Walk into any store and ask for a pair of sunglasses. Ray-Ban, Oakley, Prada, Versace, Persol. Eight different brands, eight different prices, and the illusion of competition. But every single one is owned by the same company.
This is the dark business history behind Luxottica's vertical monopoly of the global eyewear industry.
